name: Lepton flavor universality in tau decays
requirement: Any candidate framework must reproduce the observed equality of the W-boson couplings to the three lepton generations as inferred from purely-leptonic and semihadronic tau decays. The HFLAV-averaged ratios |g_tau / g_mu|, |g_tau / g_e|, |g_mu / g_e| extracted from B(tau -> e nu nu_bar), B(tau -> mu nu nu_bar), B(tau -> pi nu_tau), B(tau -> K nu_tau), and B(W -> l nu) all agree with unity at the 0.1-0.3% level, with no surviving anomaly except possibly a mild tension in B(W -> tau nu) / B(W -> mu nu) measured at LEP and reanalyzed at the LHC.

regime.description: Tau decays at energies near m_tau (B-factories, LEP, LHC). Leptonic decays are pure electroweak; semihadronic decays involve well-controlled hadronic matrix elements (V_us f_K, V_ud f_pi).

current_bound.value: |g_tau / g_mu| = 1.0009 +/- 0.0014; |g_tau / g_e| = 1.0027 +/- 0.0014; |g_mu / g_e| = 1.0019 +/- 0.0014 (HFLAV 2024 averages from leptonic and hadronic tau decays plus pi -> l nu, K -> l nu, W -> l nu)
current_bound.experiment: HFLAV global tau LFU fit (BaBar, Belle, Belle II, LEP-ALEPH/OPAL, CLEO, LHCb, ATLAS) using leptonic and semihadronic tau branchings, pion / kaon -> l nu, and W -> l nu
current_bound.reference: HFLAV Collaboration, 'Averages of b-hadron, c-hadron, and tau-lepton properties as of 2023', arXiv:2411.18639 (HFLAV 2024 update)

what_it_excludes: Constrains charged-Higgs scenarios (2HDM type-II / type-X with large tan(beta)) at tan(beta)/m_H < ~0.4 GeV^-1; vector leptoquarks coupling to taus; W' bosons with non-universal couplings. Together with R(D), R(D*) (b -> c tau nu) and R(K), R(K*) (b -> s l+ l-), tau LFU is the most precise generation-comparison test in the SM and excludes wide classes of LFU-violating BSM at the (g_tau / g_l - 1) < 0.001 level. The B(W -> tau nu) / B(W -> mu nu) ratio (LEP gave 1.066 +/- 0.025, ATLAS 2021 gave 0.992 +/- 0.013) is now consistent with universality after the ATLAS measurement; any framework predicting tau-enhanced W couplings must accommodate the modern ATLAS-averaged value.

parameterization.name: Charged-current effective couplings g_e, g_mu, g_tau in the SM Lagrangian (gauge boson - lepton vertex). Equivalently, SMEFT operators C_Hl^(3) and C_ll diagonal in lepton flavor; LFU breaking parameterized by their flavor-off-diagonal structure.
parameterization.reference: Pich, 'Precision Tau Physics', Prog. Part. Nucl. Phys. 75, 41 (2014), arXiv:1310.7922; Bryman, Cirigliano, Crivellin, Inguglia, ARNPS 72, 69 (2022), arXiv:2111.05338.
parameterization.summary: Tau LFU is tested by comparing leptonic tau branching fractions B(tau -> l nu nu_bar) (which probe |g_tau g_l|^2), semihadronic decays such as B(tau -> pi nu) / B(pi -> mu nu) (which probe |g_tau / g_mu|^2), and W decay branching ratios B(W -> l nu). Each ratio is computed in the SM with sub-permille precision after radiative corrections and known phase-space and hadronic factors.

procedural.predicted_observable: B(tau -> e nu nu_bar) / tau_tau, B(tau -> mu nu nu_bar) / tau_tau, B(tau -> pi nu) / B(pi -> mu nu), B(tau -> K nu) / B(K -> mu nu), and B(W -> l nu). Ratios constructed to cancel hadronic uncertainties.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: (g_tau / g_l - 1) at the 0.001 level for l = e, mu and the corresponding semihadronic comparisons; Wilson-coefficient bound |C_Hl^(3) flavor-off-diagonal| < few x 10^-3 / Lambda^2 (TeV^-2).
framework_inputs: If the framework introduces lepton-flavor-non-universal couplings to W (or new charged-current mediators), compute the effective g_l for each generation and confront with HFLAV averages.
Include tree-level charged-Higgs contributions to tau -> l nu nu_bar (Michel parameter rho_tau, eta_tau spectra are also constrained at the percent level).
Match onto SMEFT C_Hl^(3) or, for non-decoupled mediators, full UV completions; tools: flavio, smelli, RGEs to m_tau scale.
procedural.notes: The Pich review (1310.7922) provides the full SM analytic framework and remains the standard reference; HFLAV maintains the live world averages (2411.18639 is the most recent update). Tau LFU is comparable in precision to electron / muon mass ratios as a test of universality.

primary_references.citation: Pich, 'Precision Tau Physics', Prog. Part. Nucl. Phys. 75, 41-85 (2014)
HFLAV Collaboration, 'Averages of b-hadron, c-hadron, and tau-lepton properties as of 2023', arXiv:2411.18639 (2024)
Bryman, Cirigliano, Crivellin, Inguglia, 'Testing Lepton Flavor Universality with Pion, Kaon, Tau, and Beta Decays', Annu. Rev. Nucl. Part. Sci. 72, 69 (2022)
ATLAS Collaboration, 'Test of the universality of tau and mu lepton couplings in W-boson decays with the ATLAS detector', Nature Phys. 17, 813 (2021)
